options.systemMessage:
You are MetroMind, a friendly, professional Kochi Metro assistant on WhatsApp. Reply in concise plain text only.

### 1. Handling Location Pins
If the user ONLY shares their location (the message contains "[User location: lat, lon]" but no travel request), DO NOT call any tools. Respond: "Got your location! Where would you like to go?"

### 2. Planning Trips — MANDATORY TOOL USAGE
Whenever the user mentions ANY destination, route, direction, station, travel, trip, "take me to", "how to get to", "go to", or similar — you MUST call the Plan Trip tool. No exceptions.
- You are FORBIDDEN from planning routes, suggesting stations, estimating times, or giving directions yourself.
- CRITICAL FOR LOCATION: When calling Plan Trip, check the ENTIRE conversation history. If you see "[User location: X, Y]" in ANY previous message, you MUST include those coordinates in your query. Write your query as: "10.0717,76.3641 take me to Lulu Mall". NEVER omit known coordinates.
- The tool returns a pre-formatted response. Output it EXACTLY as received. Do NOT edit, correct, or add your own routing.
- If the tool returns an error, relay that exact error to the user.

### 3. Booking Tickets
- Collect origin, destination, passengers, and booking URL. Call Book Ticket only when all four exist.

### 4. Memory & Tool Usage (CRITICAL)
- NEVER use conversation memory or your own knowledge for routes, fares, or timings.
- ALWAYS call the appropriate tool. Memory is ONLY for context like saved locations.

### 5. Weather
After a successful trip plan, call the Weather Tool for the destination city and append a brief weather tip.

nodes.name: MetroMind AI Agent
nodes.notes: The only decision-maker. Tools are deliberately granular and descriptions define when to use them. Memory is keyed by the sender’s WhatsApp number.

parameters.description: Check if a name is a valid Kochi Metro station. ONLY use this to verify station names. DO NOT use this for general places, landmarks, or addresses (like malls, hospitals, beaches) - the Plan Trip tool handles places automatically. If the user says 'take me to X', do NOT use this tool to verify X.
Extract possible station names, passenger count, booking URL, and time expressions from a user message. Use for ambiguous booking or timing requests.
parameters.jsCode: const text=String(query.text||''); const url=(text.match(/https?:\/\/[^\s]+/i)||[])[0]||null; const passengers=Number((text.match(/\b(\d+)\s*(passenger|people|persons|tickets?)\b/i)||[])[1])||null; const time=(text.match(/\b(today|tomorrow|tonight|in\s+\d+\s+(minutes?|hours?)|\d{1,2}[:.]\d{2}(?:\s?[ap]m)?|\d{1,2}\s?[ap]m)\b/i)||[])[0]||null; return JSON.stringify({text, bookingUrl:url, passengers, timeExpression:time, stationFragments:(text.match(/(?:from|to)\s+([A-Za-z .’'-]+)/gi)||[])});
parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"text":"2 passengers from Aluva to MG Road tomorrow at 8"}

parameters.toolDescription: Check the current weather for a city only when the user directly asks about weather. Do not call this automatically after Plan Trip, and never use weather output to modify a Trip Planner route, fare, time, station, or map URL.

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { phoneNumber: $json.phoneNumber, outboundMessage: 'Sorry, MetroMind currently supports text messages only. Please send your request as a text message.' } }];

parameters.description: Book a Kochi Metro ticket. Use only when the user has provided ALL four required values: a valid booking URL (from prutech.org), an origin station, a destination station, and a passenger count. Do NOT call this tool unless all four are confirmed — never invent a missing value. Returns the booking result including a payment URL or deeplink on success, or an error message on failure.

value.destination: ={{ $fromAI('destination_station', 'The validated destination station name (e.g. Palarivattom, Vyttila)', 'string') }}
value.origin: ={{ $fromAI('origin_station', 'The validated origin station name (e.g. Aluva, MG Road)', 'string') }}
value.passengers: ={{ $fromAI('passenger_count', 'Number of passengers as an integer between 1 and 10', 'number') }}
value.url: ={{ $fromAI('booking_url', 'The KMRL booking URL from prutech.org supplied by the user. Never invent this.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: MANDATORY: Call this for ANY travel, route, direction, or trip question. You MUST NOT answer routing questions without this tool. When calling, include any known user coordinates IN your query text.

value.userMessage: ={{ $fromAI('query', 'Write the user travel request. CRITICAL RULE: If anywhere in the conversation you see [User location: X, Y] or the user shared coordinates, you MUST prepend those exact numbers to your query like this: 10.0717,76.3641 take me to Lulu Mall. NEVER skip coordinates you know about. If no location is known, just write the destination.') }}

value.latitude: ={{ $fromAI('latitude', 'The numeric latitude from the user location in [User location: lat, lon]. Extract the exact number. If no location was shared, leave empty.', 'string') || $('Execute Workflow Trigger').first().json.latitude || '' }}
value.longitude: ={{ $fromAI('longitude', 'The numeric longitude from the user location in [User location: lat, lon]. Extract the exact number. If no location was shared, leave empty.', 'string') || $('Execute Workflow Trigger').first().json.longitude || '' }}
